revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大浴場]]


{{Citation needed|date=November 7, 2016|qq=}}
[[File:GifuHotelParkOnsen2.jpg|thumb|300px|Hotel Park large bathhouse]]
'''Daiyokujō''' (大浴場) is a type of {{ill|Furo|en|Furo|qid=Q2637646|lt=bathhouse|1=Furo}}, but the term is especially used when the bathing room is spacious and can accommodate a large number of people at the same time. There is no clear distinction from a regular bathhouse, and the name is used at the discretion of the provider. Therefore, even very large bathhouses may not call themselves daiyokujō, while conversely, some medium-sized ones may.

== Overview ==
The term is mainly used for large {{ill|Hotel|en|Hotel|qid=Q27686|lt=hotel|1=Hotel}} or {{ill|day-use bathing facility|de|Tagesbad|ja|日帰り入浴施設|zh|日归入浴设施|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use bathing facility|1=1-day onsen facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11505291}} in hot spring resorts. During the {{ill|high economic growth period|de|Phase des hohen Wachstums|ja|高度成長期|zh|高速增长期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/high economic growth period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高度経済成長|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高度経済成長|1=Japanese economic miracle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1059474}}, many people traveled to hot spring resorts and lodging facilities competed in size, so as part of attracting visitors, many facilities accommodating large numbers of people were installed, and the term daiyokujō began to be used.

In the past, travelers were primarily in groups, and even individual guests had little hesitation about entering baths accommodating large numbers of people. However, as travel has become more individual, there is a trend to prefer private baths such as {{ill|family bath|de|Familienbad|ja|家族風呂|1=Family Bath|zh|家庭浴场|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/family bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Family Bath|qq=}} over large bathing areas. In addition, inns and hotels that secure abundant water supply implement {{ill|Flowing Bath|de|Flowing Bath|ja|掛け流し|zh|溢流式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}} even in their daiyokujō, but many daiyokujō use {{ill|Recirculating Baths|de|Zirkulationsbad|ja|循環風呂|zh|循环浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Recirculating Baths|qq=}}. Therefore, daiyokujō tend to be avoided by those who prefer {{ill|hot spring source|de|Quelle der heißen Quelle|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|zh|温泉水源|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hot spring source|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}}.

== Notes ==
{{Reflist|qq=}}

== See also ==
* {{ill|Public bathing|en|Public bathing|qid=Q785952|lt=Public Bathhouse|1=Public bathing}}
** {{ill|Thermae|en|Thermae|qid=Q6581615|lt=public baths in ancient Rome|1=Thermae}}
* Thousand-person bath: {{ill|Katakurakan|de|Katakurakan|ja|片倉館|zh|片仓馆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakurakan|1=Katakurakan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11569541}}, {{ill|Sukayu Onsen|en|Sukayu Onsen|qid=Q7635737|lt=Sukayu Onsen|1=Sukayu Onsen}}
* Roman-style bath: {{ill|Hotel Ōnoya|de|Hotel Ōnoya|ja|ホテル大野屋|zh|大野屋酒店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotel Ōnoya|1=Hotel Ohnoya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11337934}}
* {{ill|jungle bath|de|Dschungelbad|ja|ジャングル風呂|zh|丛林浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/jungle bath|qq=}}

{{Onsen-stub|qq=}}
